 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","title":"fcp-sdk: make schema validation limits and rate-limit retry-after env-overridable","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:29.318867Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.684405Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:25:58.684186Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["config","hardening","infra"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9fxph","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:40.914223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1955,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fcr6i","author":"jemanuel","text":"AFFECTED FILES:
- src/lib.rs: MAX_SCHEMA_ERRORS=5, DEFAULT_MAX_BYTES=256KB, DEFAULT_MAX_ARRAY_LEN=1000, DEFAULT_MAX_DEPTH=32
- src/retry.rs: DEFAULT_RATE_LIMIT_RETRY_AFTER=Duration::from_secs(30)

The schema validation limits are defensive bounds that prevent DoS via malicious payloads. They're unlikely to need tuning in most deployments but should still be overridable for edge cases (e.g., connectors that legitimately handle large arrays or deeply nested JSON).

The rate-limit retry-after of 30s is a fallback when the server doesn't send a Retry-After header. This is reasonable but should be connector-tunable since some APIs have known default rate-limit windows.

Steps:
1. Add env var support for each: FCP_SDK_MAX_SCHEMA_ERRORS, FCP_SDK_MAX_PAYLOAD_BYTES, FCP_SDK_MAX_ARRAY_LEN, FCP_SDK_MAX_DEPTH, FCP_SDK_RATE_LIMIT_RETRY_AFTER_SECS
2. Document all env vars in module rustdoc
3. Keep existing defaults unchanged","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:11:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fgot","title":"[IDEA-01.1] SDK runtime kit: trait surface + config schema","description":"## Goal
Define the minimal trait boundary and config schema for the runtime kit so streaming/polling supervisors can be used by connectors with platform-specific state.

## Details
- Add `SupervisorConfig` (backoff, jitter, retry budget, cooldown, shutdown timeout, max_consecutive_failures).
- Define connector-facing trait hooks:
  - `StreamingSession` (resume token, sequence, heartbeat state)
  - `PollingCursor` (offset/seq + persistence hooks)
- Define explicit health transition rules (Healthy → Degraded → Unhealthy) and expose `HealthSnapshot` updates.

## Tests
- Unit tests for config defaults and validation (reject nonsensical values).
- Compile-only tests in `fcp-sdk` to ensure trait ergonomics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Traits are stable enough to be used by both Discord- and Telegram-like connectors.
- Config defaults align with study docs (1s base, 60s cap, jitter on).","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add spectral routing score (effective resistance / Laplacian) as optional metric and log chosen path.
- Set gossip TTL from measured mixing-time; add convergence test that asserts bound.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Use SmallVec for usually-small object/symbol lists to reduce allocations.
- Batch symbol requests and use Bytes for zero-copy in hot paths.

Implemented SDK runtime kit trait surface + config schema in crates/fcp-sdk/src/runtime.rs:

- StreamingSession trait: resume token, sequence, heartbeat, persistence hooks

- In-memory test implementations: InMemoryStreamingSession, InMemoryPollingCursor
- Prelude exports added

**Tests:** 10 unit tests passing
**Verification:** cargo clippy/fmt/test all clean

Ready to close when blocker flywheel_connectors-1n78.20 is closed.","created_at":"2026-01-28T17:16:46Z"}]}

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fm746","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-store::MemoryObjectStore::put undercounts large headers in quota enforcement","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs:412-416 computes object_size as body.len() + 512, and crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs:449-455 enforces max_bytes against that estimate. But crates/fcp-core/src/object.rs:174-187 makes ObjectHeader carry variable-sized refs, foreign_refs, provenance, and placement, and crates/fcp-core/src/object.rs:221-239 / 287-289 allow any canonically encoded object up to MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES (64 MiB). Bug scenario: an attacker stores many header-heavy objects with huge ref lists and tiny bodies; MemoryObjectStore counts each one as only ~512 bytes even if the in-memory header consumes megabytes, so max_bytes can be bypassed by orders of magnitude and the process can OOM while quota accounting still reports plenty of headroom. Suggested fix: base quota accounting on the actual canonical header size (or canonical_bytes length) instead of a fixed 512-byte estimate, and add a regression test with a header-dominated object near the canonical cap.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:22.117426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:57:25.220283Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-fm746","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-store::MemoryObjectStore::put undercounts large headers in quota enforcement","description":"crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs:412-416 computes object_size as body.len() + 512, and crates/fcp-store/src/object_store.rs:449-455 enforces max_bytes against that estimate. But crates/fcp-core/src/object.rs:174-187 makes ObjectHeader carry variable-sized refs, foreign_refs, provenance, and placement, and crates/fcp-core/src/object.rs:221-239 / 287-289 allow any canonically encoded object up to MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES (64 MiB). Bug scenario: an attacker stores many header-heavy objects with huge ref lists and tiny bodies; MemoryObjectStore counts each one as only ~512 bytes even if the in-memory header consumes megabytes, so max_bytes can be bypassed by orders of magnitude and the process can OOM while quota accounting still reports plenty of headroom. Suggested fix: base quota accounting on the actual canonical header size (or canonical_bytes length) instead of a fixed 512-byte estimate, and add a regression test with a header-dominated object near the canonical cap.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:22.117426Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:03:22.074205Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:03:22.073690Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2835,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fm746","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit e1f7cf18.

Fix: object_size now calls StoredObject::canonical_bytes(&header, &body) and uses the real canonical-CBOR encoding length as the quota charge — same pipeline used for ObjectId derivation and the 64 MiB MAX_CANONICAL_OBJECT_BYTES cap. Fails closed on canonical_bytes() error (falls back to a 64 MiB ceiling, a local constant since fcp-cbor is a dev-dep here).

Regression test put_counts_canonical_header_in_quota constructs a header with 512 ObjectId refs (~17 KB canonical) and empty body, then asserts (a) object_size > 4 KiB, (b) new accounting dominates the old 512-byte estimate by >=8x, (c) max_bytes just below real cost rejects, (d) exact-fit accepts.

Updated pre-existing size-coupled tests to compute max_bytes dynamically from MemoryObjectStore::object_size(&probe) rather than hardcoded 512-byte math: quota_boundary_exact_fit, quota_boundary_one_byte_short, quota_exact_boundary, storage_used_accumulates_mixed_sizes, put_quota_holds_under_concurrent_inserts. They're now encoding-change-proof.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-store 803 pass, same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::evict_expired* failures (confirmed baseline-equivalent in ywpup).

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:03:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fnzgx","title":"[tooling] rch worker nightly drift triggers local fail-open after crate-scoped remote cargo","description":"Background: after the earlier RCH-E326 dependency-cycle work, crate-scoped offload from crates/fcp-kernel is now viable enough to reach remote cargo. On 2026-03-30, rch exec -- cargo check --lib from crates/fcp-kernel prepared 11 roots, finished remote sync in about 20.8 seconds, and started remote cargo on worker vmi1149989. That remote run then failed because the worker lacked toolchain nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, after which rch attempted to fail open locally. In shared multi-agent sessions this local fallback is forbidden because it can start unplanned local compilation storms. Scope: track this as a distinct tooling blocker so agents can separate it from the older path-cycle issue and choose safer verification paths. Acceptance: 1) the failure mode is explicitly tracked in beads, 2) future agents can distinguish worker-nightly drift plus local fail-open from repo metadata cycles, 3) next steps are clear whether the remedy belongs in worker fleet maintenance, rch worker selection, or repo-local guidance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-30T22:13:50.105649Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-31T02:43:56.290458Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T02:43:56.290044Z","close_reason":"Documented rch worker-nightly drift, retrieval-only failure, and unsafe local fail-open handling in AGENTS.md and README.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","title":"fcp-mesh/node: planner zones never populated (production zone-policy filtering broken)","description":"**File**: crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs:792, 801

**Code**:
```rust
fn build_planner_input(&self, now_ms: u64) -> PlannerInput {
    if let Some(profile) = &self.local_profile {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from mesh enrollment data
        });
    }
    for state in self.peers.values() {
        nodes.push(NodeInfo {
            ...
            zones: Vec::new(), // TODO: populate from peer zone advertisements
        });
    }
}
```

**Root cause**: `MeshNode::build_planner_input` hardcodes `zones: Vec::new()` for both the local node and every peer. The TODOs mark this as never being wired to real enrollment data or peer advertisements.

**Impact**: `PlannerInput → NodeInfo.zones` is threaded into `CandidateNode.zones` (planner.rs:537). Planner zone-policy filtering (planner.rs:1852 `zone_policy_rejects_nodes_with_unknown_zones`, 1806 `zone_policy_filters_candidates_by_zone`) keys off this field. In production, every candidate has empty zones, so:
- `zone_policy` set to allow only `z:work` rejects ALL candidates (no node has any zone)
- `zone_policy` set to deny `z:public` allows ALL candidates (no node is in z:public from the policy's view)
- The planner cannot enforce zone-affinity placement at all

This is silently broken: no error, no warning, just universally-bypassed filtering.

**Fix plan**:
1. Plumb local zones from the existing MeshIdentity / MeshConfig source-of-truth (likely `identity.tags` filtered through ZoneTagMapping::tag_to_zone) into local_profile or a sibling local_zones field on MeshNode.
2. Track per-peer zones from the gossip / handshake path. Peers already advertise tags via their MeshIdentity in handshake; add zone derivation alongside symbol-summary handling.
3. Replace both `zones: Vec::new()` sites with the real source.
4. Add an integration test in fcp-mesh: build_planner_input populates non-empty zones for local node when MeshIdentity has fcp tags; peer state with attested fcp tags appears in planner candidates with matching zones.
5. Add a planner-level test that demonstrates zone-policy filtering actually filters once zones flow through correctly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:39:48.973287Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.298362Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:17.297599Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit a75b9e9d: fcp-mesh/node thread local and peer zones through build_planner_input","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2798,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fqzmp","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fix landed in a75b9e9d by another swarm agent — independently arrived at the same design (local_zones HashSet on MeshNode, update_local_zones setter, both build_planner_input sites wired, same regression test shape). Verified via CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p1-t3 rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-mesh --lib plan_execution_populates_zones_from_local_and_peer_state (1 passed).","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:21:15Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","title":"Implement huggingface connector: model inference and hub API","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:10.605019Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.974513Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:50:02.974345Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1i9ky","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:14:20.587822Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1963,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-fr3x5","author":"jemanuel","text":"HuggingFace Hub + Inference API.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","title":"[FCP3/P4.1] Refactor fcp-host into a node local root app over lower layer semantics","description":"Background: fcp-host is currently the practical center of the system because it owns too much semantic meaning. In the target architecture it should remain important, but as a node local orchestrator over lower layers rather than as the source of platform truth.

Scope: refactor fcp-host so it consumes kernel, policy, evidence, and durable plane contracts instead of defining them. Supervision, orchestration, and local process management should remain in host, but execution meaning, policy meaning, and evidence meaning should come from the crates that own them. Preserve structured supervision, lifecycle, and evidence logs aligned with the lower layer vocabulary so later integration and end to end beads can compare host behavior across native, WASI, and failover paths without special case interpretation.

Acceptance: host becomes a consumer and coordinator, not the semantic owner of the platform, and host diagnostics align with the canonical lower layer vocabulary instead of reintroducing parallel meanings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SageDuck","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:06.068633Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:50:02.644739Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:16.977914Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","host","mesh-first","phase-4","refactor"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-d8jbq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:29.293485Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:30.386821Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ndoro","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:31.014754Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-vqfld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:29.848634Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2095,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"Claimed a narrow execution-control carve under this bead after repo archaeology and coordination with active agents. Current implementation moves CancelReason, CleanupBehavior, ProgressUnit, and ProgressUpdate from fcp-host-local definitions into new fcp-kernel ownership, with fcp-host re-exporting the kernel types to preserve the current host API surface while the broader node-local root app refactor proceeds.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:20:28Z"},{"id":2097,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"SageDuck","text":"Verification update: remote-only rch checking was attempted and aborted immediately on fail-open before local cargo could proceed. Verbose rch preflight reported a workspace path-cycle blocker rather than a host/kernel code error: resolver produced cyclic path dependency rooted at /Users/jemanuel/projects/flywheel_connectors/crates/fcp-core with reason code RCH-E326. Local manifest archaeology points to a real Cargo path cycle through test-only edges (fcp-core dev-dep -> fcp-testkit -> fcp-conformance -> fcp-core) plus a direct fcp-testkit <-> fcp-conformance test-helper entanglement via LogCapture. My host/kernel carve itself remains fcp-host -> fcp-kernel -> fcp-core, which is acyclic.","created_at":"2026-03-29T02:28:02Z"},{"id":2105,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented the next narrow tbjdk execution-control ownership carve. Moved host-shared semantic wire types into crates/fcp-kernel: cancellation vocabulary (CancellationOutcome, CancellationRequest, CancellationResponse, PartialResult, CheckpointInfo, CleanupResult, CancellationAuditEvent) plus progress vocabulary (PhaseTransition, ProgressNotification, ProgressPayload, ProgressOptions, AggregatedProgress). Host now re-exports those kernel-owned types from cancellation.rs and progress.rs while keeping CancellationController and ProgressController host-local. Added chrono workspace dependency to fcp-kernel and kernel-side unit coverage for moved defaults/serde stability. Local rustfmt on touched Rust files passed. Remote verification attempt was compliant-only via RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-kernel --lib; it selected vmi1156319 and got through topology preflight, but exact dependency closure sync expanded to 188 dirty roots and never reached remote cargo before I interrupted it to avoid a long-running repo-scale sync. No local cargo fallback was allowed to run.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:18:05Z"},{"id":2106,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Continued tbjdk with a second semantic-ownership carve. Moved rollout vocabulary into crates/fcp-kernel and left RolloutController plus host-specific rollout gating/runtime logic in crates/fcp-host/src/rollout.rs. Kernel now owns RolloutObservation, RolloutDecision (with public stable as_str labels), RolloutAuditEvent, RolloutEvidence (including digest), and RolloutOutcome; fcp-host re-exports them. Added blake3 workspace dependency to fcp-kernel and kernel-side unit coverage for rollout label stability and evidence digest determinism. Local rustfmt on touched Rust files passed. I did not run a second fresh RCH cargo attempt for this follow-on slice because the immediately prior compliant rch exec on fcp-kernel never reached remote cargo and was still syncing the 188-root exact dirty dependency closure when interrupted.","created_at":"2026-03-29T03:23:16Z"},{"id":2143,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"BronzeCondor taking a narrow non-overlapping tbjdk sub-slice after AGENTS/README/code archaeology. Planned change: make fcp-host consume more kernel-owned execution/lifecycle vocabulary through fcp-kernel reexports instead of importing those shared semantics directly from fcp-core, while staying off active store/GC surfaces and without changing host-local supervisor behavior. Agent Mail file reservations are currently unreliable in this repo, so coordination is being mirrored here in beads comments.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:29:34Z"},{"id":2147,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"BronzeCondor","text":"Completed a narrow host/kernel consumer refactor slice in fcp-host: doctor.rs, discovery.rs, rollout.rs, agent_api.rs, and bin/fcp-host.rs now import more execution/lifecycle semantics (ConnectorId, OperationInfo, Introspection, request/response types, lifecycle/self-check vocabulary, etc.) from fcp-kernel rather than fcp-core, while leaving core-owned policy/security types in place. No intended behavior change; this is an ownership-boundary cleanup aligned with tbjdk. Remote verification attempt was compliant-only via rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --all-targets. rch selected worker vmi1149989 but hit dependency-preflight fail-open RCH-E326 before remote cargo could run, then started a local fallback cargo check which I immediately terminated to stay within repo policy. rch status later showed the failed build as a 550ms remote-preflight failure, and worker capabilities refresh confirmed the fleet has the needed nightly, so current verification remains blocked on rch/dependency-preflight state rather than on a captured Rust compile error. Agent Mail intro/contact attempts still fail in this repo due malformed SQLite pages, so coordination is being mirrored here.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:36:32Z"},{"id":2150,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"Codex","text":"Read AGENTS.md and README.md fully, then mapped the current fwc/fcp-host/fcp-core/fcp-kernel architecture. Agent Mail remains degraded in this repo for write paths: ensure_project and list_agents succeed, but register_agent still returns 'Resource is temporarily busy', so I cannot yet introduce myself or reserve files there. To keep work moving without colliding with SageDuck/BronzeCondor, I am taking a narrow non-overlapping tbjdk slice in untouched host files: migrate remaining host-side imports of kernel-owned execution/lifecycle/resource-accounting vocabulary from fcp-core to fcp-kernel in admin_state.rs and budget.rs (and only adjacent untouched files if the same ownership cleanup is clearly local). I am explicitly staying off the currently active discovery/doctor/rollout/agent_api/bin/fcp-host surfaces and off store/conformance files already modified in the worktree.","created_at":"2026-03-31T03:50:22Z"},{"id":2153,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"Codex","text":"Progress update on the narrow tbjdk ownership-cleanup slice. Current worktree change is limited to untouched host files: crates/fcp-host/src/resilience.rs now imports ConnectorId from fcp-kernel instead of fcp-core, and crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-test-connector.rs now imports the kernel-owned execution, introspection, request, response, self-check, session, and shutdown types from fcp-kernel while leaving core-owned capability, policy, object, and risk types in fcp-core. Current archaeology shows BatchOperation carries an optional per-operation zone override, but BatchZoneValidator.validate/group_by_zone ignore it and only consult the registry or agent zone fallback. I am fixing that semantic gap by moving the override onto canonical ZoneId typing and making validation/grouping use the effective operation zone.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:44:58Z"},{"id":2212,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-01: Comprehensive acceptance verification for tbjdk closure. Evidence:

1. VOCABULARY CARVES COMPLETE: cancellation.rs, progress.rs, rollout.rs all 'pub use fcp_kernel::' for CancelReason, CleanupBehavior, ProgressUnit, ProgressUpdate, PhaseTransition, RolloutObservation, RolloutDecision, RolloutEvidence, etc.

2. HOST CONSUMES KERNEL: 24+ fcp_kernel imports across admin_state, agent_api, rollout, progress, doctor, resilience, discovery, budget, enforcement, bin/fcp-host, bin/fcp-test-connector.

3. ENFORCEMENT USES TYPED IDS: enforcement.rs now imports ConnectorId, OperationId, ZoneId from fcp_core (not raw Strings).

4. NO PARALLEL PUBLIC MEANINGS: Host-local HealthState in health.rs is private (mod health, not pub mod), marked #[allow(dead_code)], and not re-exported. Public API uses kernel HealthState exclusively.

5. CORRECT REMAINING CORE IMPORTS: 18 fcp_core imports are for security/policy types (ZoneId, CapabilityToken, CapabilityId, RiskLevel, SafetyTier, ApprovalToken, RolloutPolicy, RateLimit) that correctly belong in core, not kernel.

6. HOST IS CONSUMER+COORDINATOR: admin_state, supervisor, resilience, batch, output_capture define host-local orchestration types (process management, admin API). These are correctly host-specific, not semantic platform types.

Acceptance criteria met: host is a consumer and coordinator, not the semantic owner of the platform.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:46:10Z"},{"id":2214,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Completed a narrow batch follow-on after the bead was already closed upstream. In crates/fcp-host/src/batch.rs, BatchOperation.zone is now Option<ZoneId>, BatchZoneValidator now computes an effective operation zone so validation and group_by_zone both honor the per-operation override instead of ignoring it, and serde now rejects invalid zone strings on deserialize. In crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs, the HTTP batch planning bridge now passes the ZoneId directly instead of round-tripping through String. Local rustfmt --edition 2024 and git diff --check passed on the touched files. Remote verification used RCH_VERBOSE=1 rch exec -- cargo check -p fcp-host --tests from crates/fcp-host; remote dependency preflight verified 26 roots on worker vmi1149989, remote cargo then failed because nightly-2026-02-18-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu is not installed on that worker, and I interrupted the local fail-open fallback immediately.","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:50:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-tcp0f","title":"[SEC-AUDIT] fcp-bootstrap PKCS#11 enumeration sessions skip logout/finalize cleanup","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:569-600 opens an authenticated enumeration session with session.login(UserType::User, Some(&auth_pin)) but returns only Session, and the enumeration call sites at crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:533 and :564 tear down with session.close() only. The real cleanup path at crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:1221-1238 explicitly logs out authenticated sessions before closing and finalizes the PKCS#11 context, but enumeration never uses it. Attack scenario: certificate/key enumeration leaves the token logged in or the provider context initialized after the temporary PIN-authenticated browse step, extending the lifetime of authenticated hardware-token access for later code paths without re-prompting for the PIN. Proposed mitigation: return an RAII wrapper for enumeration sessions that carries session state plus the Pkcs11 context and always runs close_pkcs11_session(...) + finalize_pkcs11_context(...) on drop.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:16:54.069672Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:22:03.131804Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:22:03.131560Z","close_reason":"Converted open_enumeration_session to return an EnumerationSession RAII wrapper that owns (Pkcs11, Session, owns_initialization, provider_probe_lock). Both enumerate_certificates and enumerate_keys now call enumeration.close() which routes through close_pkcs11_session (logout + close) and finalize_pkcs11_context. Drop impl runs the same cleanup as a defense-in-depth backstop for panic / early-return paths. Verified: 695 lib + 143 hardware_token tests pass in fcp-bootstrap. Pre-existing test_genesis_fingerprint_matches_golden failure (golden vectors need regen post 88d515b7) is unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-tg1bp","title":"[PERF] fcp-protocol::compute_session_mac: copies every frame into a temporary buffer","description":"crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:918-944 and 1033-1045. compute_session_mac() first builds mac_input() as a fresh Vec containing session_id, direction, seq, and the entire frame payload, then feeds that temporary buffer into HMAC-SHA256 or keyed BLAKE3. This adds an extra O(frame_len) allocation and memcpy to every outbound MAC computation and every inbound verification (verify_session_mac calls compute_session_mac again), doubling per-frame memory traffic on the FCPS data path. Proposed improvement: stream the fixed header pieces and frame_bytes directly into the MAC/hash state with successive update() calls and delete mac_input() entirely.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:20.984966Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:05.073458Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-tg1bp","title":"[PERF] fcp-protocol::compute_session_mac: copies every frame into a temporary buffer","description":"crates/fcp-protocol/src/session.rs:918-944 and 1033-1045. compute_session_mac() first builds mac_input() as a fresh Vec containing session_id, direction, seq, and the entire frame payload, then feeds that temporary buffer into HMAC-SHA256 or keyed BLAKE3. This adds an extra O(frame_len) allocation and memcpy to every outbound MAC computation and every inbound verification (verify_session_mac calls compute_session_mac again), doubling per-frame memory traffic on the FCPS data path. Proposed improvement: stream the fixed header pieces and frame_bytes directly into the MAC/hash state with successive update() calls and delete mac_input() entirely.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:20.984966Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:24.443078Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:00:24.442840Z","close_reason":"compute_session_mac now streams session metadata and frame bytes directly into the MAC state without allocating a temporary buffer","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-thy","title":"fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving","description":"# fcp.twilio: SMS sending and receiving (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide core SMS/MMS capabilities:
- send outbound SMS (and MMS when enabled)
- read message status/history
- support inbound SMS via webhook-driven event flow (ingest handled in `flywheel_connectors-ugp`)

## Scope
### Outbound
- Send SMS to a single destination.
- Optional: bulk send with batching + rate limiting.
- Support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate sends.

### Status / history
- Get message details by SID.
- List messages with filters (date range, to/from).

### Media (MMS)
- List/download media attachments (bounded + host pinned).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.sms.send`
- `twilio.sms.read`
- `twilio.sms.media.read`

Policy guidance:
- Outbound send is a real-world side effect; many orgs require approval for certain destinations.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned to Twilio API host(s) and explicit media hosts.
- For sends:
  - receipts + audit events with message SID + to/from hashes (never raw phone numbers in logs)
  - strict log redaction for phone numbers and message bodies
- Taint model:
  - inbound message bodies are tainted by default

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.sms.send`
  - `twilio.sms.get`
  - `twilio.sms.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.list`
  - `twilio.sms.media.download`
- Idempotency strategy:
  - stable client idempotency key → dedupe cache/state

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send request formation
  - idempotency behavior
  - status polling
  - media download size limits
  - secret/PII redaction in logs

## Acceptance criteria
- Duplicate sends are mechanically prevented via idempotency.
- Logs and receipts never leak phone numbers or message bodies.

The existing connector already had send_message, get_message, list_messages, and download_media — these fill the media metadata gap. 49 new tests, total 268 (234 unit + 34 integration), clippy clean. Committed as 9168bedd.","created_at":"2026-03-09T08:06:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-tk9j5","title":"[CRITICAL] fcp-ratelimit buckets lose state on restart (trivial rate-limit bypass)","description":"TokenBucket (crates/fcp-ratelimit/src/token_bucket.rs:30,33), PoolState (crates/fcp-sdk/src/ratelimit.rs:103-108), SlidingWindow, and LeakyBucket all keep state purely in memory:

  tokens: AtomicU32
  last_refill: Mutex<Instant>
  count: u32
  window_start: Instant

On process restart (crash, redeploy, autoscale, OOM-kill, SIGTERM after signal handler) every pool's accumulated usage resets to zero and the window anchor resets to Instant::now(). An attacker that can trigger a connector crash -- or just waits for natural churn in a long-running fleet -- wipes the limit.

The manifest declares scope = "instance" | "credential" | "global", but all three currently enforce against the same in-memory count. "global" scope is global only within one process's lifetime.

No code path writes to $CONNECTOR_STATE or any persistent KV. Nothing is plumbed through fcp_host state persistence either.

Fix shape:
  - Checkpoint {count, window_start} per pool behind the tracker lock (debounced write)
  - Load on RateLimitTracker::from_declarations keyed by scope+pool_id
  - Host-level cross-process aggregation for "global" scope is a separate design (mesh gossip or host-backed counter service)

Security impact: this is the single clearest admission-control bypass in the repo. Any rate limit a manifest author declares can be reset by killing the connector.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:57:53.160982Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:44:06.874533Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:44:06.874260Z","close_reason":"Persisted fcp-sdk ratelimit checkpoints across restart; added restart and scope-key regression tests; commit 35c7d499","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-tr2xx","title":"[Perf] Verify performance targets from README: cold start <100ms, invoke <2ms, memory <10MB, binary <20MB","description":"The README specifies concrete performance targets: cold start <100ms/<500ms (p50/p99), local invoke latency <2ms/<10ms, Tailnet LAN invoke <20ms/<100ms, symbol reconstruction 1MB <50ms/<250ms, secret reconstruction <150ms/<750ms, memory <10MB per connector, CPU <1% idle. NONE of these have been measured or proven. The project has benchmark infrastructure (criterion benches in fwc and fcp-core) but they measure search/schema/PCS operations, not the end-to-end performance targets. This initiative creates benchmark harnesses for each target and records baseline measurements. Depends on E2E initiative because invoke latency requires a running fcp-host.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:52:54.910877Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-07T18:19:51.629840Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T18:19:51.629615Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","perf"],"comments":[{"id":2585,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-tr2xx","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose: All 6 children closed. Performance verification complete: cold-start, invoke latency, memory/binary size, symbol reconstruction, secret reconstruction benchmarks all established. CI benchmark gate added to ci.yml.","created_at":"2026-04-07T18:19:50Z"}]}
